title: session: Inconsistent max session name length validation
description: CRITICAL: Session name validation has inconsistent max length. Evidence: isolate_core::domain/identifiers.rs line 290 uses > 63 (max 63), but isolate::session.rs line 162 uses <= 64 (max 64). This creates confusion and potential security issue.

title: sync: Missing --dry-run in session sync
description:
BUG: The --dry-run flag exists in deprecated 'sync' but not in 'session sync'.

## Evidence
- cargo run -- sync --dry-run works
- cargo run -- session sync --dry-run returns error

## Expected
Both commands should have --dry-run flag

## Actual
Only deprecated command has --dry-run

## Severity
P1 - Missing feature parity

title: sync: Exit code 0 despite failures
description:
BUG: Exit code is 0 even when sync fails.

## Evidence
- Ran: cargo run --quiet -- session sync
- Output shows: "Synced 1 session(s), 1 failed"
- Exit code: 0

## Expected
Non-zero exit code when any sync fails

## Actual
Exit code 0 despite failures

## Severity
P0 - CI/CD pipelines will not detect failures

title: CRITICAL: isolate MCP tools broken - wrong binary installed
description:
The isolate MCP tools are calling a binary that doesn't exist or has wrong interface.

## Finding
- MCP tool isolate_focus calls: isolate focus test-session --no-zellij --json
- System has isolate binary: version 1.0 (sandboxing tool)  
- The sandboxing tool doesnt support focus, --no-zellij, or --json flags
- Error: Unknown flag: --no-zellij

## Test Results
1. Focus active session: Exit 1 - Unknown flag
2. Focus paused: Same error
3. Focus non-existent: Same error
4. Focus failed: Same error

## Root Cause
MCP tools expect session manager but binary is sandboxing tool with same name but different purpose.

## Severity
P0 - CRITICAL: All isolate MCP tools non-functional

title: session-remove: Locked sessions bypass contract and are removed
sync: JSON output doesn't match documented contract
description:
BUG: The --json output format doesn't match the documented contract.

## Evidence
- Ran: cargo run --quiet -- sync --json
- Help says output should be single JSON with schema:
  {"": "isolate://sync-response/v1", ...}
- Actual output: Multiple JSONL lines

## Expected
Single JSON response per contract spec

## Actual  
Multiple JSONL lines (action + summary + result)

## Severity
P1 - Output format contract is broken

title: sync: Dirty workspace sync succeeds without warning
description:
BUG: The session sync command succeeds even when there are uncommitted changes in the workspace.

## Evidence
- Created uncommitted changes in test-sync workspace
- Ran: cargo run --quiet -- session sync test-sync
- Output: Success message even with dirty workspace
- No --allow-dirty flag exists in session sync

## Expected
Either error on dirty workspace OR require --allow-dirty flag

## Actual
Sync succeeds with dirty workspace

## Contract Spec
According to contract-spec.md P3: 'Workspace must be clean (no uncommitted changes) OR caller explicitly acknowledges dirty state'

## Test Case
1. Create uncommitted change in workspace
2. Run: cargo run -- session sync test-sync
3. Expected: Error or warning about dirty workspace
4. Actual: Success

title: isolate: Panic on config subcommand
description: The 'isolate config list' and 'isolate config get key' commands cause the CLI to panic with 'unable to spawn process: Exec(2)' instead of showing a proper error or help message. This is a critical bug - the CLI should never panic on user input.

title: doctor: JSON output schema mismatch
session add: Name with only hyphens fails at CLI not validation
description: MINOR BUG: Session name '---' (only hyphens) is interpreted as CLI flags rather than a validation error. Test: isolate session add '---' shows 'unexpected argument ---' instead of validation error explaining name must start with letter.

title: session add: Output not valid JSONL without --json flag
description: MINOR BUG: Without --json flag, output is mixed log lines + JSON, not valid JSONL. Test: isolate session add test --no-open produces log lines (timestamps, INFO) then JSON objects. This breaks JSONL parsing. With --json flag, output is valid JSON envelope.

title: sync: Inconsistent --all flag between commands
description:
BUG: The --all flag works with 'isolate sync' but not with 'isolate session sync'.

## Evidence
- Ran: cargo run --quiet -- sync --all
- Output: Works (with deprecation warning)

- Ran: cargo run --quiet -- session sync --all
- Output: error: unexpected argument '--all' found

## Expected
Both commands should support --all flag consistently

## Actual
Only the deprecated command supports --all

## Severity
P1 - Inconsistent CLI behavior

title: session add: Names starting with hyphen show confusing CLI error
description: MAJOR BUG: Session names that look like flags cause confusing CLI errors. Test: isolate session add -test fails with 'unexpected argument -test' rather than validation error. Expected: proper validation error with explanation.

title: session list: --bead filter always returns empty
description: The --bead filter in isolate session list always returns 0 results. It filters by session.metadata.bead_id which is never set.

title: isolate: Uses expect() causing panic on spawn failure
description: In isolate-0.1.2/src/main.rs:162, the code uses .expect() when spawning a process. When the process cannot be spawned (e.g., in container environments without namespace support), this causes a panic instead of a helpful error message. The error 'Exec(2)' means 'No such file or directory' but users see 'thread panicked at unable to spawn process'.

title: sync: --dry-run executes sync anyway
description:
BUG: The --dry-run flag does NOT actually do a dry run - it executes the sync anyway.

## Evidence
- cargo run --quiet -- sync dryrun-test --dry-run
- Output shows "Would sync workspace..." BUT ALSO performs actual sync
- Output shows "Synced session 'dryrun-test' with main" action

## Expected
Dry-run should ONLY preview, not execute

## Actual
Dry-run executes the sync anyway

## Severity
P0 - Users relying on dry-run for safety will have their workspace modified

## Impact
This is a data-integrity bug that can cause unexpected workspace modifications

title: sync: Missing --contract flag in session sync
description:
BUG: The --contract flag exists in deprecated 'sync' but not in 'session sync'.

## Evidence
- cargo run -- sync --contract works
- cargo run -- session sync --contract returns error

## Expected
Both commands should have --contract flag

## Actual
Only deprecated command has --contract

## Severity
P1 - Missing feature parity

title: session-remove: Uncommitted changes ignored during removal
session add: Missing --idempotent flag compared to add command
description: MAJOR BUG: Inconsistent CLI behavior between add and session add. The 'add' command supports --idempotent flag but 'session add' does not. Test: isolate add test --idempotent works, but isolate session add test --idempotent fails with CLI error. This is inconsistent UX.

title: session list: --agent filter always returns empty
description: The --agent filter in isolate session list always returns 0 results. It filters by session.metadata.owner which is never set. This is the same root cause as the --bead filter bug.

title: cli: isolate status crashes with panic
description: The isolate status command crashes with panic 'unable to spawn process: Exec(2)' for all scenarios: no session, invalid session, and valid session. Should return proper error messages and exit codes instead. Reference: zjj status handles this correctly.

title: doctor: Inconsistent JSON schema across commands
